name: Madrid (Spain, city)

colors.symbolism: the historic carmine of the city's arms

description: A carmine-red field bearing the city's coat of arms at the centre: a bear reaching up to a strawberry tree, beneath a royal crown.
history: Adopted by Madrid City Council on 13 December 1967; the bear-and-strawberry-tree (Oso y el Madrono) emblem dates from the 13th century. Distinct from the flag of the Comunidad de Madrid (the autonomous community), which is red with seven white stars.

features.role: Coat of arms of Madrid: a bear (oso) reaching up to a strawberry tree (madrono), with seven stars and a royal crown
features.symbolism: the bear and the madrono tree symbolise the city of Madrid; the seven stars form the constellation Ursa Major
